zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles II de Lorraine於1364年9月11日出生在法國洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希時，他的父親John I Duke of Lorraine18歲，母親Sophie von Württemberg21歲。他於1393年2月6日與Margarethe von der Pfalz在德國貝也安格結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1431年1月25日在他的家鄉享年66歲，埋葬在法國洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles II de Lorraine于1364年9月11日出生在法国洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希时，他的父亲John I Duke of Lorraine18岁，母亲Sophie von Württemberg21岁。他于1393年2月6日与Margarethe von der Pfalz在德国貝也安格结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1431年1月25日在他的家乡享年66岁，埋葬在法国洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lorraine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Perrine, Pierre.

French and English: habitational name from Lorraine, a region in the northeastern part of France. Its name derives from the name of the medieval kingdom of Lotharingia, which in turn was named for its sovereign Lothar (a personal name composed of the elements hlūd ‘famous, renowned’ + hari, heri ‘army’).
Altered form of French   Lorrain  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Loring
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French Lohereng ‘man fromLorraine’ (see Lorraine ).

variants.name: Lorrain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Alban, Andre, Desire, Fernande, Philippe, Pierre.
French: variant of   Lorraine  . This surname is also found in Britain. Compare   Laurain   and   Laurin  .
History: Pierre Lorrain dit Lachapelle from Angliers in Vienne, France, married Françoise Hulin in France c. 1656.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles II de Lorraine was born on 11 September 1364, in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France, his father, John I Duke of Lorraine, was 18 and his mother, Sophie von Württemberg, was 21. He married Margarethe von der Pfalz on 6 February 1393, in Amberg, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 25 January 1431, in his hometown, at the age of 66, and was buried in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles II de Lorraine est né le 11 septembre 1364, à Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France, son père, John I Duke of Lorraine, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Sophie von Württemberg, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Margarethe von der Pfalz le 6 février 1393, à Amberg, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 25 janvier 1431, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles II de Lorraine am 11. September 1364 in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater John I Duke of Lorraine 18 und seine Mutter Sophie von Württemberg 21. Er hat am 6. Februar 1393 in Amberg, Bayern, Deutschland Margarethe von der Pfalz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Januar 1431 im Alter von 66 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lothringen, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles II de Lorraine è nato il il 11 settembre 1364 nel Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia, il suo padre, John I Duke of Lorraine, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Sophie von Württemberg, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Margarethe von der Pfalz il 6 febbraio 1393, nel Amberg, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 25 gennaio 1431, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles II de Lorraineが1364年9月11日にフランスロレィヌメウルスエ-エト-モセルナンシで生まれた時，父John I Duke of Lorraineは18歳，母Sophie von Württembergは21歳であった。1393年2月6日にドイツバイエルンアンバーグでMargarethe von der Pfalzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1431年1月25日に故郷で66歳で死去し，フランスロレィヌメウルスエ-エト-モセルナンシにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles II de Lorraine(이)가 1364년 9월 11일 프랑스 로레이인 우르테-트-모셀 난시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John I Duke of Lorraine(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophie von Württemberg(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1393년 2월 6일 독일 베이른 아버그에서 Margarethe von der Pfalz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1431년 1월 25일 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 로레이인 우르테-트-모셀 난시에 묻혔다.

pt.11 September 1364–25 Jan 1431: 11 de setembro de 1364–25 de janeiro de 1431
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles II de Lorraine nasceu em 11 de setembro de 1364, em Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, França, seu pai, John I Duke of Lorraine, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Sophie von Württemberg, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Margarethe von der Pfalz em 6 de fevereiro de 1393, em Amberg, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de janeiro de 1431, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles II de Lorraine родился 11 сентября 1364 г. в местности нанси, меурте-ет-моселл, лорреаин, Франция, его отцу по имени John I Duke of Lorraine было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Sophie von Württemberg, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Margarethe von der Pfalz 6 февраля 1393 г. в местности эмбирг, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 25 января 1431 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности нанси, меурте-ет-моселл, лорреаин, Франция.

es.11 September 1364–25 Jan 1431: 11 de septiembre de 1364–25 de enero de 1431
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles II de Lorraine nació el 11 de septiembre de 1364, en Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, John I Duke of Lorraine, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Sophie von Württemberg, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Margarethe von der Pfalz el 6 de febrero de 1393, en Amberg, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 25 de enero de 1431, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.
